title: Composition, inheritance and super

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Composition, inheritance and super; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: The Python Tutorial; Python 3.14; Tutorial chapter 9: scopes, classes, instances, inheritance, private conventions, and iterators; atomic topic: Composition, inheritance and super
sources.claim: Python inheritance should represent substitutable behavior with cooperative super calls, while composition delegates work without exposing an unnecessary subtype relationship.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Python inheritance should represent substitutable behavior with cooperative super calls, while composition delegates work without exposing an unnecessary subtype relationship.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Two mixins use cooperative super with compatible signatures so every initializer in the MRO participates exactly once. Your goal is to implement or refactor a small design using justified composition or inheritance. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Scope and closures; Modules and imports; Classes, instances and attributes. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Python inheritance should represent substitutable behavior with cooperative super calls, while composition delegates work without exposing an unnecessary subtype relationship. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Multiple inheritance uses method resolution order, super means next in that order rather than a named parent, signatures and invariants must remain compatible, and fragile base state can couple subclasses. The learner must reject this documented misconception: super always calls one fixed parent class and inheritance is the best reuse mechanism whenever methods overlap.

includes: Prefer composition and use inheritance only for substitutable behavior with cooperative initialization.
Implement or refactor a small design using justified composition or inheritance.
excludes: Multiple inheritance uses method resolution order, super means next in that order rather than a named parent, signatures and invariants must remain compatible, and fragile base state can couple subclasses.

outcomes: Implement or refactor a small design using justified composition or inheritance.

examples.title: Composition, inheritance and super: direct language case
examples.situation: Two mixins use cooperative super with compatible signatures so every initializer in the MRO participates exactly once.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: Two mixins use cooperative super with compatible signatures so every initializer in the MRO participates exactly once.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Use inheritance only for substitutable behavior and follow the method resolution order cooperatively; prefer composition when reuse does not require a subtype.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Use inheritance only for substitutable behavior and follow the method resolution order cooperatively; prefer composition when reuse does not require a subtype.

examples.title: Composition, inheritance and super: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A Stack owns a deque rather than inheriting from it, preventing callers from using queue operations that violate the stack contract.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A Stack owns a deque rather than inheriting from it, preventing callers from using queue operations that violate the stack contract.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Multiple inheritance uses method resolution order, super means next in that order rather than a named parent, signatures and invariants must remain compatible, and fragile base state can couple subclasses.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: super always calls one fixed parent class and inheritance is the best reuse mechanism whenever methods overlap.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement or refactor a small design using justified composition or inheritance.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: super always calls one fixed parent class and inheritance is the best reuse mechanism whenever methods overlap.
misconceptions.correction: Use inheritance only for substitutable behavior and follow the method resolution order cooperatively; prefer composition when reuse does not require a subtype.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: super always calls one fixed parent class and inheritance is the best reuse mechanism whenever methods overlap.

analogy.example: a family of service records whose instance state, generated value methods, inheritance order, and protocols define observable behavior can introduce Composition, inheritance and super, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: classes, data classes, composition, inheritance, and special methods
limitations: descriptor lookup, MRO, hashing, NotImplemented, and shallow frozen state do not map cleanly to physical records
